meta.title: SIG-AE-1: Semantic Modeling, Writeback, and Governed Delivery

domains.name: Semantic Layer Design and Input Tables

questions.stem: Why do Sigma teams publish reusable base datasets?

options.text: To create a governed semantic layer that multiple workbooks can reuse
To replace every workbook with CSV exports

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because reusable datasets centralize business logic and support self-service analysis. **B**, **C**, and **D** contradict the purpose of a semantic layer.

questions.stem: What is the main difference between a base dataset and a derived dataset in Sigma?

options.text: A derived dataset builds on another dataset's logic, while a base dataset starts closer to the source
A base dataset can never be shared
A derived dataset cannot contain calculations

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because derived datasets inherit and extend prior modeling logic. **B**, **C**, and **D** are false.

questions.stem: Why document columns with descriptions and tags in Sigma?

options.text: To help users understand definitions and find trusted fields more easily
To convert workbooks into templates automatically

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because metadata improves discoverability and semantic clarity. **B**, **C**, and **D** are unrelated.

questions.stem: A semantic layer should balance explorer flexibility with governance. Which design choice best supports that balance?

options.text: Publish trusted datasets for reuse while still allowing derived analysis in downstream workbooks
Prevent all analysts from creating new calculations
Force every workbook to query raw tables directly

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because trusted reusable datasets provide guardrails without eliminating exploration. **B**, **C**, and **D** either overrestrict or weaken governance.

questions.stem: Which TWO practices strengthen centralized metric governance in Sigma? (Select TWO)

options.text: Define KPI logic once in reusable datasets
Use certified or endorsed datasets for common reporting
Allow every workbook author to redefine revenue differently
Remove data dictionary information from the workspace

questions.explanation: **A** and **B** are correct because centralized definitions and certification improve consistency. **C** and **D** undermine governance.

questions.stem: What problem does a data dictionary solve in a Sigma workspace?

options.text: It explains field meaning and usage so analysts can self-serve more safely

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because dictionaries help users interpret fields correctly. **B**, **C**, and **D** are unrelated.

questions.stem: Why can long inheritance chains of derived datasets become a maintenance risk?

options.text: Changes upstream may have broad downstream effects that are harder to trace
They prevent all calculations from running
They force every workbook to be public

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because deep dependency chains complicate impact analysis and troubleshooting. **B**, **C**, and **D** are not inherent consequences.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of an input table in Sigma?

options.text: To let authorized users enter or update workbook data directly for a controlled use case

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because input tables support governed writeback scenarios such as plans or annotations. **B**, **C**, and **D** are incorrect.

questions.stem: A finance team wants budget owners to enter forecasts beside warehouse actuals in the same workbook. Which Sigma capability best fits?

options.text: Input tables combined with warehouse-backed data in the workbook

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because input tables support manual entries alongside warehouse data. **B**, **C**, and **D** do not solve the writeback requirement.

questions.stem: Which type of calculation is best suited to a rolling 12-month average?

options.text: A window-style calculation over an ordered time series

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because rolling averages depend on ordered analytic windows. **B**, **C**, and **D** are unrelated.

questions.stem: Why would an analyst use a CASE-style expression in Sigma?

options.text: To implement multi-branch business logic in a calculation

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because CASE logic branches based on business conditions. **B**, **C**, and **D** are not calculation use cases.

questions.stem: A workbook needs year-over-year growth by region while preserving the region partition. What kind of logic is required?

options.text: A time-aware window or period-over-period calculation partitioned by region

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because year-over-year analysis depends on ordered partitioned calculations. **B**, **C**, and **D** are unrelated configuration tasks.

questions.stem: Which TWO functions are most associated with statistical or analytical calculations rather than access control? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: **A** and **B** are correct because percentile and standard deviation are statistical functions. **C** and **D** are administrative or delivery features.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of a cross-element lookup in Sigma?

options.text: To reference a value from another workbook element or related calculation context

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because cross-element references let one element reuse values computed elsewhere. **B**, **C**, and **D** are unrelated.

questions.stem: Why are ordered partitions important for many window functions?

options.text: They define the sequence and grouping over which the analytic result is computed

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because window functions depend on partition and order semantics. **B**, **C**, and **D** do not describe analytic window behavior.

questions.stem: An analyst wants to compare each salesperson against the median performer in the same region. Which approach best fits?

options.text: Use grouped statistical calculations such as percentile or median logic by region
Move all data to an input table

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because comparing to a regional median requires grouped statistical calculations. **B**, **C**, and **D** would not solve the analytic task.

questions.stem: Why tag a workbook version before a major release?

options.text: It creates a named checkpoint that is easier to review or roll back to later

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because version tags support release management and rollback. **B**, **C**, and **D** are unrelated.

questions.stem: What is the value of a template workbook in Sigma?

options.text: It standardizes reusable reporting patterns for future work
It forces every chart to use the same color

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because templates speed repeatable development with consistent structure. **B**, **C**, and **D** are false claims.

questions.stem: Why review change history after an unexpected workbook regression?

options.text: It helps identify what changed and whether a prior state should be restored

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because history supports troubleshooting and rollback decisions. **B**, **C**, and **D** are not what change history is for.

questions.stem: What is the main governance benefit of promoting a workbook from draft to published to certified status?

options.text: It signals increasing trust and review maturity to downstream users
It converts all formulas to SQL

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because maturity states communicate review level and trust. **B**, **C**, and **D** overstate or misstate the purpose.

questions.stem: Which TWO actions support reusable development in Sigma? (Select TWO)

options.text: Copying approved elements between workbooks when appropriate
Using template workbooks for standard patterns
Deleting all change history after each publish

questions.explanation: **A** and **B** are correct because reusable elements and templates improve speed and consistency. **C** and **D** weaken maintainability.

questions.stem: What is the difference between Sigma-managed credentials and customer-managed service accounts at a high level?

options.text: They represent different ways the warehouse connection can authenticate
One is for charts and the other is for tables only

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because the distinction concerns connection authentication and control. **B**, **C**, and **D** are inaccurate.

questions.stem: Why might an organization prefer OAuth or identity-passthrough-style access patterns for some connections?

options.text: They can align warehouse access more closely with the end user identity
They prevent input tables from working

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because user-level identity patterns can preserve downstream access boundaries. **B**, **C**, and **D** are false.

questions.stem: What is required for Sigma writeback features to persist changes into a warehouse table?

options.text: The connection and target permissions must allow the write operation
A map visualization must be present
The workbook must be exported first

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because writeback depends on the underlying connection and target-table permissions. **B**, **C**, and **D** are unrelated.

questions.stem: What is the main technical concern with cross-connection joins?

options.text: They may have added complexity or performance considerations because data comes from multiple connections
They remove the need for datasets

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because cross-connection analysis often increases complexity and can affect performance. **B**, **C**, and **D** are incorrect.

questions.stem: Which TWO operational tasks matter for healthy warehouse connectivity? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: **A** and **B** are correct because secure, reliable connectivity depends on monitoring and credential hygiene. **C** and **D** do not improve connection health.

questions.stem: Why certify a dataset in Sigma?

options.text: To indicate it is a trusted, approved source for common analysis
To make it impossible to filter
To delete the underlying warehouse table

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because certification signals trust and approval. **B**, **C**, and **D** are not effects of dataset certification.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of SCIM provisioning in analytics platforms?

options.text: To automate user and team lifecycle management from an identity provider

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because SCIM automates provisioning and deprovisioning workflows. **B**, **C**, and **D** are unrelated.

questions.stem: Why should embed implementations be governed carefully?

options.text: They can expose analytics to external audiences, so data access boundaries must be deliberate
They convert input tables into charts
They remove the need for permissions

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because embedding can extend access beyond internal authors, making governance crucial. **B**, **C**, and **D** are false.

questions.stem: A workbook is shared widely, but some users should edit input rows while others should only view results. What is the best control principle?

options.text: Separate write permissions from read permissions with least privilege
Give everyone full edit rights for simplicity
Move the workbook to a hidden folder only

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because least-privilege write controls are essential for governed input-table scenarios. **B**, **C**, and **D** either overgrant or fail to address the requirement.

questions.stem: Which TWO controls commonly contribute to secure sharing in Sigma? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: **A** and **B** are correct because effective sharing depends on both content permissions and underlying data access. **C** and **D** do not improve security.

questions.stem: What is a scheduled report primarily used for?

options.text: Delivering workbook results on a schedule through channels such as email or messaging integrations

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because scheduled delivery sends report outputs to users automatically. **B**, **C**, and **D** are unrelated.

questions.stem: Why might a team use a webhook delivery for workbook outputs?

options.text: To integrate report delivery with an external workflow or system

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because webhooks support automation and integration. **B**, **C**, and **D** are not the purpose of webhook delivery.

questions.stem: What is the main difference between sharing a workbook and certifying a dataset?

options.text: Sharing controls distribution of content, while certification signals trust in a data asset

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because distribution and trust signaling are distinct governance concepts. **B**, **C**, and **D** are incorrect.

questions.stem: A company wants to expose a customer-facing embedded workbook but only with a narrow, approved slice of data. What matters most?

options.text: Design the embedding and underlying permissions so exposed data is intentionally limited
Increase the number of workbook tabs

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because embed governance is fundamentally about controlling what data is exposed. **B**, **C**, and **D** do not address the risk.

questions.stem: Which TWO statements about governed sharing are most accurate? (Select TWO)

options.text: Effective sharing depends on both the workbook permissions and underlying data access
Certified datasets help consumers trust reused content
Slack delivery automatically grants data-source access to everyone
Scheduled exports remove the need for access review

questions.explanation: **A** and **B** are correct because distribution and trust must align with underlying access controls. **C** and **D** are unsafe assumptions.

questions.stem: Why might an operations team use an input table for annotations?

options.text: To capture human notes or adjustments directly in the analytic workflow
To replace all warehouse fact tables
To stop users from filtering data

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because input tables are useful for manual enrichment such as comments or notes. **B**, **C**, and **D** are not valid reasons.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of a correlation-style statistic in an analytics workbook?

options.text: To estimate the strength of relationship between variables
To publish a report on a schedule

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because correlation statistics measure relationships between variables. **B**, **C**, and **D** are unrelated.

questions.stem: Why should release managers avoid publishing major workbook changes without a named version or checkpoint?

options.text: Rollback and audit become harder if the release causes problems
The workbook can no longer connect to data

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because change control depends on being able to identify and revert a prior state. **B**, **C**, and **D** are not inherent consequences.

questions.stem: What is connection health monitoring intended to catch?

options.text: Issues such as broken credentials, unavailable backends, or operational problems with a connection
Whether a workbook has enough labels

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because connection monitoring focuses on reliability and access health. **B**, **C**, and **D** are unrelated.

questions.stem: Why is it risky to assume workspace permissions alone are enough to secure data?

options.text: Underlying connection and warehouse access also influence what data can actually be queried
Workspace permissions override every other control automatically

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because analytics-platform sharing must align with underlying data-system permissions. **B**, **C**, and **D** are false.

questions.stem: What is the most likely reason to send scheduled workbook results to Slack or email?

options.text: To distribute timely insights to stakeholders without requiring them to open the workbook each time

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because scheduled delivery reduces friction for routine consumption. **B**, **C**, and **D** are unrelated.

questions.stem: Which TWO design choices usually improve self-service while preserving governance in Sigma? (Select TWO)

options.text: Provide trusted published datasets with clear metric definitions
Add descriptions and tags so fields are understandable
Force every user to query raw warehouse tables directly

questions.explanation: **A** and **B** are correct because trusted reusable datasets and strong metadata support safe self-service. **C** and **D** weaken usability or governance.

questions.stem: An analyst wants week-over-week comparison for each product family. What additional modeling detail matters most?

options.text: The calculation needs the correct time ordering and product partitioning
The workbook must be certified first

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because comparative time calculations depend on the right partition and order. **B**, **C**, and **D** are unrelated prerequisites.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of approval workflows around certification or endorsement?

options.text: To signal that a dataset has been reviewed before broad reuse
To turn all metrics into writeback tables

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because approval workflows help teams identify trusted reusable assets. **B**, **C**, and **D** are not the purpose.
